checks.a_analysis: The output contains AI-generated table captions and summaries which are explicitly permitted. No fabricated prose or invented body text was found. All section titles and paragraphs correspond to headings and text found in the source document.
There is no contact information (phone numbers, emails, or mailing addresses) in the source document or the output. No URLs were found to mismatch.

The 'NC Counties' subsection within Appendix B (found on page 9 of the source) is entirely missing from the output. This section contains a critical grid of the 100 counties in North Carolina. Additionally, while the pipeline generated its own structure, the content of Page 2 (Table of Contents) is not present.
checks.b_evidence_source: NC Counties list on page 9, containing 100 counties starting with 'Alamance' and ending with 'Yancey'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The output for Section 12 (Appendix B - Domains) skips the county list entirely and begins with the 'Place Type' table.

checks.a_analysis: Two primary tables in the document are severely truncated. The 'Appendix A - Fields' table in the source spans 5 pages (pages 4-8) and contains 45 data rows; the output table in Section 11 contains only 9 data rows (missing 80% of the content). The 'Place Type' table in the source spans 5 pages (pages 9-13) and contains 54 data rows; the output table in Section 12 contains only 22 data rows (missing 59% of the content). Both exceed the 20% threshold for a Major defect.
checks.b_evidence_source: Appendix A - Fields table (pages 4-8) with 45 data rows; Place Type table (pages 9-13) with 54 data rows.
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 11 table has 9 rows; Section 12 'Place Type' table has 22 rows.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections 11 and 12, corresponding to Pages 4-8 and 9-13

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Sections 11 and 12 are missing substantial amounts of their intended content due to the truncation of the primary data tables and lists they were meant to contain. Specifically, Section 11 is missing four out of five pages of field definitions, and Section 12 is missing the county list and the first three pages of place type classifications.
checks.b_evidence_source: Full content of Appendix A (pages 4-8) and Appendix B (pages 9-13).
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 11 and Section 12 are significantly truncated compared to the source material.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text that was successfully converted maintains high fidelity to the source. No meaning-altering changes were found in the Preface, Introduction, or the portions of the tables that were present.

checks.a_analysis: The rendered HTML significantly deviates from the source PDF's visual organization. Entire sections of textual content from page 3 (Preface, Introduction, Definition, General Data Requirements) are missing. Furthermore, the visual flow is disrupted as the document jumps from the title page information directly into a truncated table from Appendix A, omitting nearly five pages of the document's main content and data tables.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 3 of the source PDF contains comprehensive text sections titled 'AddressNC Data Content Standard', 'Preface', 'Introduction', 'Definition', and 'General Data Requirements'. Pages 5-8 contain the bulk of the 'Appendix A - Fields' table. Page 9 contains the 'NC Counties' list.
checks.b_evidence_output:
The HTML document entirely omits the text from page 3, the table rows from pages 5-8, and the list of NC Counties from page 9. The heading '

AddressNC Data Content Standard

' is immediately followed by a partial table, losing the original document's structure.
checks.b_evidence_location: Main content area, following the committee names list.

checks.a_analysis: The tables in the HTML are severely truncated and misrendered relative to their structure in the source PDF. The 'Appendix A - Fields' table (spanning pages 4-8 in the PDF) is rendered as a small 9-row fragment. Similarly, the 'Appendix B - Domains (Place Type)' table (spanning pages 9-13 in the PDF) is missing its first three pages of content, starting only with the 'Religious' row found on page 12 of the original.
checks.b_evidence_source: The 'Appendix A - Fields' table starts on page 4 and ends on page 8 with the 'UniqueID' field. The 'Appendix B - Domains' table starts on page 9 with 'Agricultural'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML table for Appendix A stops at 'St_PosDir'. The HTML table for Appendix B starts at 'Religious', omitting 'Agricultural' through 'Residential - Student Housing'.
checks.b_evidence_location: Tables with captions 'AddressNC Data Content Standard Fields and Domains' and 'Domain Attribute Descriptions for Residential and Other Classifications (Continued)'.

checks.a_analysis: The primary meaningful image, the map of North Carolina on page 1, is present in the HTML output. Decorative watermarks like 'DRAFT' are correctly marked as presentation/decorative per accessibility guidelines.
There is no evidence of visual content duplication within the body of the rendered HTML.
